name: Hungarian Cucumber Salad (Uborkasaláta)
intro: A classic Hungarian side salad of paper-thin cucumber slices, salted and squeezed dry, then dressed in a light sweet-sour vinaigrette with garlic and paprika. The perfect cool accompaniment to rich mains like paprikás csirke.
method: Using a mandoline slicer, slice both cucumbers as thinly as possible into a bowl. Add the salt and mix the cucumber slices to lightly and evenly coat them. Let sit for 15 minutes to draw out the water.
Take large handfuls of cucumbers and, with your hands clenched, squeeze out as much liquid as you can. Place the squeezed cucumbers in a clean bowl. Discard the liquid.
In a cup, combine the sugar, vinegar, and 1 cup of cold water. Stir until the sugar is dissolved. Pour this mixture over the squeezed cucumbers and mix thoroughly.
Add the minced garlic, sprinkle with paprika powder and ground black pepper. Serve chilled.

servingSuggestions.name: Ungarisches Paprikahuhn
servingSuggestions.notes: The traditional pairing — this salad cuts through the rich paprika sauce
